target.name: Delian Alliance Industries

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Delian Alliance Industries is a Seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $14M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 firms (2 public, 8 private), including same-sector peers at Seed and Series A stages: Tenna Systems ($60M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Stark Defence ($500M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $14M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next funding stage, implies a valuation of $35M. Peer-median analysis across the four comparable companies yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in Series A stage and company maturity within the defense sector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $30M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $130M. The wide band reflects structural uncertainty: the Stage-Progression method anchors conservatively to capital deployed, while the Peer-Median approach captures the upper-end revenue and growth multiples seen in Series A-funded competitors. Key drivers of final valuation will be product-market fit evidence, revenue traction, and technical differentiation relative to the peer set, particularly relative to Tenna Systems at the Seed stage.
